title: As a shopper, I want to manage my shopping cart so that I can review and adjust my items before checkout

acceptance_criteria.given: a shopper adds a product to their cart
acceptance_criteria.then: the cart icon shows the updated item count and the item appears in the cart drawer with name, price, quantity, and subtotal

acceptance_criteria.given: a shopper adds the same product twice
acceptance_criteria.then: the quantity increments by 1 rather than creating a duplicate line item

acceptance_criteria.given: a shopper changes the quantity of a cart item to 0
acceptance_criteria.then: the item is removed from the cart

acceptance_criteria.given: a shopper has items in their cart
acceptance_criteria.when: they close the browser and return within 7 days
acceptance_criteria.then: their cart items are still present

acceptance_criteria.given: a cart item's stock drops below the quantity in the cart
acceptance_criteria.then: a warning appears showing the new stock level and quantity is adjusted to maximum available

acceptance_criteria.given: a shopper views a non-empty cart
acceptance_criteria.then: they see the order subtotal, estimated tax, and a Proceed to Checkout button

non_functional_requirements.security: Cart stored server-side for authenticated users. Guest carts in Redis with 7-day TTL. Prices validated server-side at checkout.
non_functional_requirements.accessibility: Quantity inputs have labels. Remove buttons have aria-labels. Cart total announced to screen readers on update.

definition_of_done: Stock validation at view time verified
